fields.title: Python for Data Science & AI
fields.description: Learn Python from scratch. Covers data manipulation, NumPy, Pandas, and intro to Machine Learning with real-world projects.

fields.title: Full-Stack Web Development Bootcamp
fields.description: Build real-world projects with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js and MongoDB. Beginner to advanced coverage.

fields.title: Business Management Fundamentals
fields.description: Introduction to core business concepts. Topics include organizational behavior, strategic planning, and management principles.

fields.title: Accounting & Financial Statements
fields.description: Covers journal entries, ledger accounts, balance sheets, P&L statements and corporate taxation basics.

fields.title: Data Structures & Algorithms in C++
fields.description: Master arrays, linked lists, trees, graphs, sorting, dynamic programming â€” everything needed for placement interviews.

fields.title: React.js â€” Complete Guide 2025
fields.description: Build modern SPAs with React hooks, Context API, Redux, React Router. Includes 3 project builds.

fields.title: Digital Marketing Masterclass
fields.description: SEO, SEM, social media strategy, email marketing and analytics. Includes Google Analytics certification prep.

fields.title: Database Management with SQL
fields.description: Master SQL from zero to advanced â€” SELECT, JOINs, subqueries, indexing, stored procedures and transactions.

fields.title: Corporate Finance & Investment
fields.description: Valuation, capital budgeting, risk & return, portfolio management and financial statement analysis for commerce students.

fields.title: Machine Learning with TensorFlow
fields.description: Build neural networks, CNNs, RNNs and NLP models with TensorFlow 2.x. Includes image classification and sentiment analysis projects.

fields.title: Python & Data Science Test
fields.description: Test your Python and data science fundamentals.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to create a DataFrame in Pandas?
questions.explanation: pd.DataFrame() is the constructor for creating DataFrame objects in Pandas.

questions.question: What does the `len()` function return for a list?
questions.explanation: len() returns the number of items in a list.

questions.question: Which library is primarily used for numerical computing in Python?
questions.explanation: NumPy provides fast array operations and mathematical functions.

questions.question: What is the output of `print(type([]))` in Python?
questions.explanation: [] creates a list object, so type([]) returns <class 'list'>.

questions.question: In Python, which keyword is used to define a function?
questions.explanation: The 'def' keyword is used to define a function in Python.

fields.title: Web Development Fundamentals Test
fields.description: Test your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript knowledge.

questions.question: Which HTML tag is used to link an external CSS file?
questions.explanation: <link rel='stylesheet'> is used to import external CSS.

questions.question: What does CSS stand for?
questions.explanation: CSS stands for Cascading Style Sheets.

questions.question: Which JavaScript method is used to select an element by its ID?
questions.explanation: getElementById() returns the element with the specified ID.

questions.question: What is the correct way to create an arrow function in JavaScript?
questions.explanation: Arrow functions use the () => {} syntax introduced in ES6.

questions.question: Which CSS property controls text size?
questions.explanation: font-size controls the size of the text.

fields.title: Business Management Test
fields.description: Test your knowledge of core management concepts.

questions.question: Which management function involves setting organizational goals?
questions.explanation: Planning involves defining goals and determining the best way to achieve them.

questions.question: SWOT analysis stands for?
questions.explanation: SWOT = Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats â€” a strategic planning tool.

questions.question: What is the primary goal of Human Resource Management?
questions.explanation: HRM focuses on recruiting, managing, and developing employees.

questions.question: Which leadership style gives employees maximum freedom to make decisions?
questions.explanation: Laissez-Faire leadership provides minimal direction, giving full autonomy to employees.

questions.question: Marketing mix refers to the combination of?
questions.explanation: The 4 Ps of Marketing Mix are Product, Price, Place, and Promotion.

fields.title: Accounting & Finance Test
fields.description: Test your accounting and financial statement knowledge.

questions.question: Which financial statement shows a company's financial position at a specific point in time?
questions.explanation: The Balance Sheet shows assets, liabilities and equity at a specific date.

questions.question: What is the accounting equation?
questions.explanation: The fundamental accounting equation: Assets = Liabilities + Owner's Equity.

questions.question: Which of these is a current asset?
questions.explanation: Accounts Receivable is a short-term asset expected to be converted to cash within a year.

questions.question: Depreciation is applied to?
questions.explanation: Depreciation is the reduction in value of fixed assets over their useful life.

questions.question: When a business pays rent in advance, it is recorded as?
questions.explanation: Rent paid in advance is a prepaid expense â€” a current asset.

fields.title: DSA Fundamentals Test
fields.description: Test your data structures and algorithms knowledge.

questions.question: What is the time complexity of Binary Search?
questions.explanation: Binary Search divides the search space in half each time â€” O(log n).

questions.question: Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In, First Out) order?
questions.explanation: A Stack follows LIFO â€” the last element pushed is the first to be popped.

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of QuickSort?
questions.explanation: QuickSort has O(nÂ²) worst case when the pivot is always the smallest/largest element.

questions.question: Which traversal visits nodes in Left â†’ Root â†’ Right order?
questions.explanation: Inorder traversal: visit left subtree, then root, then right subtree.

questions.question: A hash table lookup has average time complexity of?
questions.explanation: Hash tables provide O(1) average-case lookup using a hash function.

fields.title: SQL Database Test
fields.description: Test your SQL and database fundamentals.

questions.question: Which SQL clause is used to filter records?
questions.explanation: WHERE filters rows before any grouping occurs.

questions.question: What does JOIN do in SQL?
options: Combines rows from two or more tables
questions.explanation: JOIN combines rows from related tables based on a condition.

questions.question: Which aggregate function returns the total number of rows?
questions.explanation: COUNT() returns the number of rows matching the specified criteria.

questions.question: What is a PRIMARY KEY?
options: A key that can have NULL values
A column that uniquely identifies each row
questions.explanation: A PRIMARY KEY uniquely identifies each record and cannot be NULL.

questions.question: Which command removes all rows from a table without deleting the table?
questions.explanation: TRUNCATE removes all rows efficiently without logging individual deletions.
